reviews.review_text:
- Begin playing 
- love the game 
- get trinkets
- buy dlc
- watch your favorite hero die 
- watch your party die
- cry
- go on 10 moth break
- rediscover game 
- load game 
- watch your favorite hero die again because you left after it happened 
- cry 
- be sad
- start new save repeat 
11/10 would recommend
Great game, love the lovecraftian world setting!

The game isn't difficult-- it's a waste of time. The game acts like it is just really challenging but really any setbacks from losing heroes can be easily overcome by spending hours grinding other heroes up to that level through easier quests. And that is just not something I want to do. Other hard games like elden ring can be overcome by getting better, but this game must be overcome by grinding heroes up and dumping them into the challenges until the RNG allows a breakthrough. I have better things to do.

If you enjoy farming low level dungeon x5-10 times before you're able to run a single high level dungeon, then this game is for you.
The game has numerious time waster mechanics and systems that serve no purpose other than to push you into farming for gold, curios, trinkets and characters. 

It rather alarming that it's common concensus amongst fans of DD1 to defend these problems and point you towards a treatmill with joy and glee as if the monotonious task of repeating dungeons over and over to keep up with gold drain is a reward. 
There are many games that the entire loop of repeating an activity is enjoyable because of the challange or depth of gameplay leading to that goal. DD1 loop is neither rewarding or enjoyable. Combat is very single solution based, targeting rear stress dealers and stunning, no matter your battle or dungeon it boils down to the same rotation and team setup. Surprisingly this is a held oppinion by the same fans who defend the monotonous gold farming, so I am perplexed.

You've got a team you want, enter a dungeon, three scenarios play out
- You suceed, you'll have to sort out any bad/positive quirks and/or diseases, each of these make the character unavaliable for a week each and cost gold. You end up with zero gold after sorting all that. 
- You fail (retreat), all the above + have to deal with stress which takes an additional week and gold.
- You fail (death), trinkets equipped are lost, the character need to be replaced, which requries you to grind another up.

Curing stress has a chance to make your characters unabliable for +2-3 addtiional weeks, more time wasted. 
Trinkets are completly rng, many are extremtly vital as enemies become exceedingly harder to hit, hit harder, are tankier and faster, trinkets are how you bridge this disadvantage. 
Quirks are passive buff/debuffs that can make a huge difference and are completly rng, it's the difference if you get surprised (randomises your team position), land or avoid a hit and take less stress damage. 
Characters at certain resolve levels will refuse to enter lower level dungeons, characters that enter dungeons above their level recieve stress damage and these dungeon locations/difficulty are rng, while each dungeon requires a specific make-up of characters to tackle the advantages enemies have. You'll reach a point you delete your entire roster realizing these problems and start the "real" progression. 

The few good things, which aren't related to the gameplay itself are the art style and narriration, they go extremly well together and are very iconic. Unfortunately there hasn't been anything else that I can say the same for.

Overall the gameplay is exhausing and feels more like a chore, which is the last thing any developer should be aiming for.
The Voiceactor alone keeps this game in my favourites. The strategy itself is also competently done, though it does require patience and determination to get through it all.

This campaign is not for the faint of heart.
Darkest Dungeon perfectly captures the feeling of pursuing an impossible quest. The game is demanding, brutal, and stressful. Beyond all however it is extremely well made. Every part of the game is immersive and intriguing. I have never felt so conflicted about a game before, as there is a lot to overcome when learning how to even get past a single mission without losing a hero or spending a week healing your party. After finally getting to the Darkest Dungeon I have realized what a masterpiece this game is. I highly highly recommend to anyone who enjoys a challenge(and doesn't mind a lot suffering along with it).

Overall a challenging game and you will go through pain before figuring out a route to success. Even then, you eventually get hit with harder challenges and it can still go wrong when you think you are in control. I suppose that is the attraction of such games, the unknown and the eternal search for perfection.
Repetitive, repetitive, repetitive. When you are done with that grind some more just to lose your built characters to RNG.

Hail fellow adventurer! Do you want to slay roving bands of monsters inspired by Lovecraftian horror? Are you ready for your cortisol levels to spike? Are you ready for high risk, high reward moments?! Play this game!!! If you have already read The Devils by Joe Abercrombie, you will genuinely enjoy it!!

First off, I believe people who complain about RNG concerning the combat have no idea how deep the preparation phase is. 
To give them some leeway, this game does not explain ♥♥♥♥, and I wish I was exaggerating to how much it throws you in the deep end. Probably my biggest critique is how there are barely to none hints for early game. You either set yourself for a run or watch all of that time wasted because you had no idea that speed was correlated to initiative. Plus the added bonus is that you don't see numbers go up and farm that sweet sweet dopamine like most roguelikes/dungeon crawlers. You have to do it old fashion way by sitting down and using math yourself. Which again could of been a non issue if things were not only explained, but shown on the UI. 
GOD do I love this game though, it has an amazing art style and sound track. The main gameplay loop is pretty damn entertaining and I appreciate the fact that it doesn't get stale near the end game. If anything it picks up the intensity of not wanting to watch your crew, that you had spent time growing pretty attached to, get brutally slammed by the random ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ collector that had no business appearing at the end of the hallway. I still have a lot more gameplay ahead of me, but so far this game is an easy recommend for me. 
ALSO, genuinely why tf is non of the stats explained?? It's super concerning that I have to watch a 45 minute video just to understand what ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ speed does.
Difficult and dark, but so so fun. Really worth a play.
The game is really difficult, but addictive as hell.

Seems fun if you like hard roguelike games, and has great art and atmosphere.
But too hard and feels too luck dependent, not for me.

Whether you should play this game depends on how masochistic you are and how much of your social life you are ready to sacrifice. If you do not want to make any of these sacrifices, well, too bad—your destiny was or will be sealed from the moment you launched this game.

You know how even if you live a super healthy lifestyle, balance work and social life, make plenty of money working in a field that you love you can still just trip down the stairs and break your neck if you are not careful at the right time? Resulting in you living for 30 years instead of a projected 95?

This game really likes to remind you that.

Good looking bad game. I guess some masochist might like it. I don't like games where you do everything you can to survive and still some random enemy takes down your whole party without you having any chacne to even run avay. It's just pointless waste of time and mental energy.
not a fan of turnbased combat, but this... hot damn. i also really REALLY love this artstyle, its giving DREDGE.

This game is the most difficult, stressful, annoying game I've ever had the misfortune of discovering, first on my playstation, now on here. 10/10, I recommend everyone, including you, yes, YOU THE READER, to play it

Egregious saving system with steam cloud implementation, no amount of good game design or personal favours towards this game can save me from giving this review. Hours lost, unclear saving, these are egregious.

I really wanted to like this one. It's got great character art, great turn based combat and 2D dungeon crawling. It's just not forgiving regardless of the mode you pick, I picked the easiest mode hoping for a more chill experience. Instead I found myself losing my favorite character that was crit by different enemies in two rounds. My character was above the level recommendation too. It's not worth the wasted time and frustration IMO.
addictive RNG nonsense, plasters its own version of "losing is fun" at the start just to make you feel like you aren't crazy for playing it.
One of the Best games I've ever played. Highly recommend for anyone interested in turn based RPGs. You can probably run it on a potato and it'd still be amazing.

I've quite enjoyed building and training my army, which is always a nice reprieve from all the battling. I've found myself thinking of plots to do better in the dungeons and quite drawn back for another go. The battles themselves have a complex strategy that can make your team a finely tuned unit.
Its very addictive. Great value for the time spent playing. Dnd but quick and dark!

Good game with annoying difficulty.
You can get wiped out in minutes.
Regardless it is addictive.
Stress Simulator. Lovely art direction, lovely AND IINFURIATING gameplay. And an absolutely pristine narration. Rest in Peace, Wayne June, you will be sorely missed.
Good game, got it yesterday and I already have 6 hours lol
Goated game, mods, hours of pain and suffering. Truly phenomenal 10/10
great game! movement system promotes good teambuilding intuitively and plenty of content, great modding community. confused on the amount of NSFW mods tho

The boring RNG grind gets old and despite the great music and narration, the other aspects are just reskinned boring battles over and over fueled by RNG rolls, kind of like playing a slot machine, yes there is a basic level of "strategy" to curating your roster and their abilities for each mission, but the grind is just boring and the actual missions are just roll after roll and deciding when to push and when to take a loss, its a great concept but the gameplay is just too boring and grindy.

The most ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ game of all time, I'll miss something with 95 accuracy three times in a row on a one hp monster with 0 dodge as they spam skills that increase my entire parties stress until they all have heart attacks.
Excellent gameplay and atmosphere

The game isn't hard, it's just annoying. There are a lot of things about it that i want to like, and I really really tried to like this title. I will come back to it because I just feel like I am missing something.

Here is my reason I do not recommend it: Half of the time the game is just playing itself, and games where you don't get to actually play the game is terrible game design, it just becomes an auto battler and I do not want to spend money on a game to not really play it. The stress mechanic is obnoxiously unforgiving, My heroes only die to this, I can keep them alive easily, then they stress out and start dying to themselves or attacking my other hereoes and this is what makes the game un-fun. I make a huge effort to keep my heroes healthy then they stress out and lose all their HP, it's not easy to heal them back up, so your effort is wasted.

The other thing is the stress recovery, 1 hero can basically do 1 mission then they need to recover for 1, but likely 2 runs to really be ready to run them again. I want to run with the heroes I want, and find synergies between my heroes, not this.

I don't know, I am torn on this title. I enjoy the dungeon crawling and the thrill of it, but the challenge is very artificial. Plus, then the game playing itself that is a huge turn off for me, I despise this about any game that has this kind of mechanic. Mechanics where an enemy stuns me and I lose a turn, totally fine, but when my hero has a turn and burns it attacking another of my heroes or just passes their own turn, like wtf is this?!

I am a sucker for upgrading things in a game, and the upgrade paths are interesting to me, so I will come back and check it out later, I don't know why, but I have an itch to keep playing it. I love challenging games, and ones that are brutally difficult, but there are 2 kinds of difficult, and this is not he good kind, so far with my experience. There is something here, but as of right now, i am not seeing it. I really hope I can come back and revisit this review and make it positive, because I don't like leaving negative reviews as people put a lot of time into these kinds of projects and I care about that, so I try and be constructive to some degree.

Enjoying losing repeatedly to this game. This reminds me fondly of how much I enjoyed playing Final Fantasy Tactics poorly (and still do. Desert Island game.) Only a couple of dozen games in, but feels like there is a huge framework of gameplay in both depth and replay value. First game to tear me away from my 3 year Rimworld obsession.

Play this game. You will try, you will fail, you will learn, and you will try again.

This certainly isn't a roguelike, don't believe the tags. I'd compare it to a nuzlocke, if you're a pokemon fan.
Heroes come and go, get stronger, tough it out, learn new moves, and die forever. Different fights require different strategies to complete, such as overwhelming power, targeting entirely one enemy, or chipping away at the enemy with statuses. Managing your roster and keeping everyone healthy and alive is difficult but satisfying. I personally love the Death's Door mechanic. I've not seen other games do anything quite like it, and it's a very fitting addition here.

Anyways, back to the pit

lots of fun!!! I am yet to understand the whole extent of the mechanics but it is really fun and the core gameplay loop is addicting, even if it can be frustrating at times. Still, pretty dope gameplay and peak aesthetics!!

It is unique backhanded praise that the feeling of dread is so strong I can never bring myself to reopen this game, remembering all the parties of characters I spent days with all meeting a frantic and gruesome end in seconds.
Goated game. I don't even like turn based games except for Expedition 33 which lets you parry. As a fan of lovecraftian stuff this game is amazing

really good game, lepers piss me off, lot of unnecessary porn mods
10/10 game
5/10 mods
I'm new to the franchise. I legit just got the game a few days ago, and it's really fun as someone who enjoys D&D type of stuff.

This game is amazing. The atmosphere and art is unmatched, the mechanics are great, the story is perfection. I definitely love it, but I also found it exhausting. It's a lot of managing characters and even more grinding, all while also keeping an eye on your town and deliberating the risk of specific missions. I cannot emphasize how much grinding it is enough - it is *so much*. Again, I very much enjoyed it, but even now I'm too much of a coward to fully finish it now that I have a team I'm attatched to, and the thought of having to raise more high level teams seems daunting.

I think the DLC is worth it as well, but be warned, it's much harder than the base game in some aspects and can change gameplay a lot.

Overall, beautiful game and worth the price, just keep in mind the commitment the game wants you to make.

It's a grind, and death is inevitable, but if you like squad based tactics and management this is your game. I actually came back to this after years of owning it and never getting very far when I was looking for experiences similar to Xcom 2/LW. It scratches a certain itch for punishment, pain, and moments of valor in hopelessness. Definitely worth a try if any of that interests you; also, has great mod support!
Great game, havent played much, but iv seen a friend play it before and decided to try it myself, i really like the art style and the atmosphere with the gameplay.

I would highly recommend this games to nearly every. About 2 years ago I mentioned to my fiance and she told me it didn't look fun. I finally got her to play it and she put about 35 hours worth of game play since she started and says she enjoys it. Ask for guides and team builds to make, personally i own on every console and device I have.

This is not a game it is a challenge, a challenge of just how much you as a player can endure. This game is unforgiving but that is what makes it so good. the tough nature of the game paired with the consequence of failure and death make this the best single player game i have ever played hands down. 10/10
Fun as ♥♥♥♥. Brutal as hell. Read up guides and still ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ inted. There's all kind of mods to add new classes to the game.

This gives me vibes of this old game I used to play in the 90's and early 2000's called Haven, but more complex. I enjoy the exploration style and that with each new run you learn better methods for the game. Easy to get lost in and all around enjoyable for me.

Its a grind, but in the grind you find the joy. I don't have much time on Steam in this game, but I've put in hundreds of hours on Playstation, particularly my Vita, and I'd heartily recommend this to anyone who loves turn-based tactical fights and some of the best atmosphere there is in video games

♥♥♥♥ this game. This game is like cutting your ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ arm off because it got infected and then you realize it was the wrong arm. I've had moments in this game that making having the flu look fun. This game pisses me off so hard that I can feel the years drain from my life and my hair turn fully gray as I get hit with 26 damage crit seven time in a row on the same person and they deathblow on the first chance they get because they went crazy and decided to hit themselves. 10/10 would recommend.

This game has many unique mechanics. Stress, sickness, light, positive and negative quirks,etc. At first, these mechanics may feel overwhelming but after a while they all come together under a common theme. And that theme is; grim. 

I feel like you will either love or hate this game. You can see that from other reviews, many players have either 50+ hours or less than 30 mins in the game.
The game is pretty dark maybe even one of the darkest games, with very dark dungeons. It's kind of like a Darkest Dungeon® ...

A Rouge like that has amazing art style, and monster design. I usualy get to frustrated at games like this that are unforgiving, but the game play loop is very fun and would recomend to any one looking for a dark and lovecraftian rouge like

I have beaten the game on its default difficulty (Darkest). I can give this game a solid 7.5 to 8 out of 10. Its a fun little turn based strategy game where you play with math and the worlds dice rolls to get the outcome that isn't you crying over loss leveled characters and trinkets. I have spent the last 3 days grinding out newly leveled level 6 characters because the end game wont let you take the same characters into the ( Darkest Dungeon ) more than once ( its easiest difficulty (Radiant) removes this ) . I wouldn't really say that was fun at the end I simply just wouldn't let this game beat me at the end. another thing I would say is if you don't care about achievements ( I did ) download a mod that increases the animation speeds and the walking speeds. more than half of this game is waiting on animations and walking through halls. Have fun hating your life ( I did ). Its a great game!
My graveyard is full and my pockets are empty. Great game, it’s like I’m playing real life.
Hey, this looks like such a cozy little game. I can’t wait to come home from work, play it, and relax.

I like the depth of the atmosphere, the music, the characters, the story, and the mood. The game is difficult, but the difficulty makes the game much more thrilling, as the stakes are high and seem real. It's absorbing, exciting and fun.
Pretty much impossible to win but way better than Darkest Dungeon 2.
Lots of darkness and samey atmosphere. Difficult, stressful, a bit boring.

Please keep in mind when reading this review that I haven't played this game in years.  I'm 99% certain I got this when it was in early access, because I distinctly remember the update where slain enemies started leaving corpses behind.  I tried the update out at the time, decided I didn't like it, found out about the public backlash, and then waited.  Eventually an update came out that made that feature optional.  I'm really glad that option was created, because I'm one of the people that actually used and appreciated it.

Anyway, regarding my overall opinion of the game, I think it's good but I have no desire to ever play it again, at least not without looking into mods that could shake things up.  See, what I really like is building up the settlement and heroes, grinding missions for resources, minimizing bad traits, that kind of progression.  I played until I was able to attempt the final mission, failed a few times, and got so upset over losing some great heroes I just didn't feel like grinding some more heroes up enough to give the final mission a third try.  I'm sure I could've beaten it eventually but I just lost interest in doing so, especially when I'd already experienced the parts of the game I liked best.

As for the DLC content, I remember it being a mixed bag and not much else.  I do recommend the game, I did have plenty of fun with it, and I think I'd give it a 7.5~8/10 score.  Oh, the narration is really iconic and the music isn't bad at all either, gotta mention that!
its pretty good, the correct amount of difficulty, a bit many gooner mods however
amazing voice actor, amazing game, amazing everything
Me has estresado, alegrado, trolleado, esperanzado, entretenido, disgustado pero siempre estas cuando te necesito te amo Darkest Dungeon

Incredible art, sound, voice, dialogue, and overall world design.
Gameplay is decent enough, the base gameplay of turnbased party organization is good.
They do fumble the ball on some things, like being heavy RNG based, I thought I was going crazy or misunderstanding mechanics like how food works, or why the enemy took 4 turns before I got 1.

From a game design standpoint it has some heavy flaws, and its a shame the second game isnt just this wiht those flaws fixed.

I was surprised to find out the bugs I experienced were fairly widely experienced due to them using their own engine made from scratch, and rather than fix them, its up to the player to avoid them.

The artistic design and decent gameplay carry it enough to get past most of the bad design and bugs.
Some of the game ideas played with have inspired many other games, so that in itself makes this game very special.

This is one of those games where I start playing and tell myself I'm going to just run a couple of dungeons and then hours have gone by. Excellent game, love the strategy, the RNG can be quite annoying though so be prepared for an RNG that doesn't even believe you exist lmao. Overall: 9/10

This one is a tough one to appreciate. i went from hating it to appreciating it, and finally loving it. it's such a punishing game (initially) that it turns into a grind. but like any puzzle it can be solved. 

most notably, it's understanding the curios and their keys (you are not meant to engage with all of them, they quickly end you runs if you blindly engage in them all), and learning to control the stress is vital to ending the grind. i found the jester class critical to ending the grind. once i built my teams around that class, the only time i picked up stress was boss battles.

once you solve the stress issue, negative quirks arrive MUCH slower and you can more easily remove them in time at the hamlet. also you don't spend all your time in the stress relief buildings, which means you save money which means you can buy more upgrades, etc. solving the stress has so many knock on effects, that it ends the grind.

so yes, in summation, this is a game with a steep learning curve that can easily leave you thinking it can only be grind. but there are solutions, and there is satisfaction is playing smart and building your teams which end the grind and let you focus on more on enjoying the game.

but as always, buy on on discount, as its the sort of game which will easily have you throw your hands up in frustration and give up. good luck.

Do not be fooled. This is a dark fantasy business game where you manhandle your employee's sanity and juggle the ethical conundrum of exploiting their own motivations for the sake of continuing forth on your selfish quest for that oh so sweet ancestral inheritance. Mods give this game tons of hours of playability and if you so desire a game that's rewardingly difficult, then this might just be for you. Is it hard? Yes. Is it satisfying? Yes.
hard journey through the dungeons of the atmpospheric dark fantasy world and whatever, but WHY is there so many porn mods

really love this game. At first I was really overwhelmed by all of it and the stress system really frustrating. but it's really fun to play. like other roguelike games "just one more run" really hit me hard with this games.

Fantastic game. Timeless graphics. Not too many systems but still challenging and you'll be surprised by how attached you get to your favorite heroes before they inevitably die. The game is under 3gb and could run on a potato. 10/10.
A little intimidating at first but really fun, can see myself grinding this game for hours
Fun game that I keep coming back to time and again. Buuuuut I have never once properly beaten it XD

4/5 Really iconic and gritty game. Good balance and variety. Wonderful art style and narration as well. The oppressive atmosphere honestly made me have to slow down how often I played the game. Sometimes I felt as though I were being driven as mad as my characters. still good fun though

Listen, is the RNG skewed against you 9 out of 10 times? Yes. But its very much like Dark Souls or Bloodborne. Once you get good, you get GOOD. It can be really fun when you find a team that just works perfectly with each other.

Spooky freaking game that can actually hurt you psychologically if you don't notice the narrator's digs at you, so legit be careful. Otherwise, very good game, especially if you like dark or edgy themes. It's not easy in the beginning, but once you get used to it, it's fine really. A-bomb and flagellant all the WAAYYY!!

Fantastic art, frustratingly addictive in the way only a roguelike can be. I love the occult aesthetic, the character archetypes, and the nastiness of the enemies. The tilemap design of the dungeons is old-school in a way i love as well.

Super fun game, challenging with deep strategy. Can be frustrating with strings of misses followed by the enemy critting you to pieces, but it's not called Joyful Dungeon, right? The random crashes are bizarre, but at least the game saves you right where you are every time. For sure recommend in every case.

It is recommended only for those who enjoy suffering. This game absolutely loves to wipe its feet on you. It even made me go against my own principles and feel hatred towards myself. Overall, it was a truly gruelling experience that I probably won’t ever revisit, but it was certainly worth trying.

This game introduced me to Lovecraftian literature, and for that I am very grateful. 
As for the game itself, what else is there to say other than it's nearly perfect? If somehow my review is the first one you read, you will lose heroes, you will lose missions, it is inevitable. The game teaches you to accept loss and fight on in spite of it all. 
Lastly it would not be as good without the voice of Wayne June (RIP)

Great turn based game. You might have harsh time but as you learn, things get better. Really fun mechanics like choosing different party members, equipping your characters with new and better gears, changing their skills in a way you want and upgrading them. Game give you chance to clean negatives you get. Overall fun things to do. I played this game twice maybe I will return for the third time later. I didn't play the second game so I can't compare them. Story teller voice and the way he talk also great ;)
Game is 3GB and I have modded another 3GB with anime ♥♥♥♥. Best modding experience! Love it working perfectly on linux as well!

Good game overall, but there are a 2 things I don't like.
1. having a level cap of 6 for each character makes things remain unnecessarily difficult for the entire game. It means that no matter how long you play the game, you can never get to a point where it feels easy. I'm a firm believer that if you spend enough time grinding in a game, you should be rewarded with an equal amount of power, so I hate it when games do this. Capping the amount you can upgrade skills and weapons is fine, but I think you should be able to level to around 8 and gain stat bonuses to HP, crit chance etc.
2. The trinkets, even at the rarest level, have singnificant debuffs to counter the buffs they give. This also seems unnecessarily punishing to the point where even after 50 hours, I find myself sometimes prefering common trinkets that don't have debuffs. Very rare and ancestral should be clearly the best and should offer power that I want badly enough to search for multiple.

Outside of that I suppose I don't have any real complaints. Yes, it can be a very frustrating game, but that's part of the point, so I can deal. And I don't mind permenantly losing characters if they die because it makes sense. A lot of the systems like the stress accumulation and stress relief in the game make sense. It's unique and an interesting experience.
Fantastic game. I love the mechanics. The variety of characters and abilities gives you a lot of room to experiment. The style and theme are pulled off rather well. One of my favorites.
Great game, excellent way to relieve stress, and focus on the eldritch horrors that both haunt and protect you.

"These Nightmarish Creatures Can Be Felled! They Can Be Beaten!"

This game definitely falls into the category of: “I absolutely love it… but I don’t want to play it.”

This year I finally gave it a proper try. And you know what? It’s not for me. But the game itself is amazing.

The visuals, art style, soundtrack, and especially the narrator’s voice are absolutely incredible. You can tweak some settings to make the experience a bit easier, but even then, this is definitely not a game for casual players.

Your heroes are essentially meat for the grinder. There’s no real point in getting attached to them. After a few expeditions, you quickly learn who’s worth saving, healing… and who will end up in the tavern, the abbey, or the graveyard.

The core gameplay is simple — turn-based combat — but the game is brutally difficult and incredibly depressing. In my case, only about one in five expeditions ended successfully. The rest was just bones rattling.

Your “heroes” fall into depression, masochism, hallucinations, or selfishness. And those are just the better outcomes. What awaits you in the darkness is far worse.

And yet, I genuinely enjoyed the game. I still come back to it from time to time for a run or two through the swamps or dungeons.

But the truth is, even after about 8 hours, I know I cannot finnish this game. Still, I had a good time, and its visual and gameplay style is truly unique and brilliant.

"BACK TO THE ABYSS!"

Rating: 7/10

My little brother got me to play this game and I really enjoyed it!
I would have gone for 100% if not for the Butcher's Circus.

This is probably the most stress inducing game I own. It has probably taken off at least 6 months of my lifespan, yet it is still one of the best games I've played in my life. Every minute I play this game feels like hours due to the amount of stress it causes. I've had so much fun while playing this while being on the verge of a stress induced coma. I highly recommend buying this game.
Could I just play the ♥♥♥♥♥♥ game?  I mean I've never played a game that's so challenging just to play it.
The game has become almost impossible to finish as you progress towards the end.

You're always 30s of bad rng away from thoroughly wasting hours of progress

It's a great game to watch other people playing and suffering through it. Don't play it yourself if you respect your time at all, because this game sure as hell doesn't

I'd say for the first maybe 14 hours, this game had me HOOKED. It felt all the ways the devs probably wanted it to feel: had me really weighing out risk vs reward during basically every second of gameplay, both losses and gains felt impactful and appropriately devastating and exhilarating. I was addicted to the loop going from dungeon excursions to town and back. 

But then my enjoyment dropped off a cliff after that. What happened? I think a lot of the fun comes from uncertainty, and not knowing what you will encounter in a dungeon, and it makes it feel riskier to go in and harder and more satisfying to get a good team / gameplan for uncertain circumstances. But there's only 5ish dungeons total, and only so many enemy types in each dungeon, and only so many effective party compositions... so basically, figuring out how to play the game effectively is the fun part, but once you've figured it out it suddenly becomes a lot less fun. 

In fact the thing that killed it for me was that I defeated a certain boss, which I thought was very cool, had some unexpected tricks up her sleeve, it was a tough fight and we barely made it out alive. yada yada all the GOOD stuff, right? Only to discover later that in order to progress through that dungeon and therefore through the game, I had to beat that SAME boss again, just this time at a higher level. Beating it the first time? Exciting! The second time? Tedious. Especially because if the boss killed my characters who were at higher levels, I'd have to spend time GRINDING my replacement characters up to that level in order to progress. 

So I think this game really only has like 15 hours of content and discovery (maybe the final dungeon is different but still), but the campaign is padded out to like 40 hours by making use of difficulty scaling aka higher numbers and necessary grinding. So it went from extremely addictive and fun to suddenly very boring and tedious. An odd experience.

i guess the game just isnt my cup of tea , having the possibility of a character getting worse as they gain xp is very anoying you end up scraping a lot of heroes because fixing them dosnt make sense economically, this plus finding out the other bosses in a region are just reskins of the first ever boss in it kinda killed my intrest in the game.

Endelss, super moddable. one of the few games of this decade that has no pity for the players XD  
I love it, its really good, love the style, the gore, the grim-dark style of it all

Can't recommend. Combat feels low-agency and boring and early progression is a slog. Tone is 'dark' and feels like it's trying really hard to be edgy with no narrative or character-driven support for that feeling. Gameplay is slow; long animations, slow-walking characters, unnecessary splash screens, etc. make playing the game not feel smooth. I know many people say this game is a masterpiece; that was not my experience.

After playing hours of this game, I find it to be extremely overrated. This game is not exactly challenging despite setbacks of losing characters since I get that's part of the game and depending on your party build, but it's just extremely time consuming and a waste of time, like some ripoff mobile phone game where all you do is constantly grind in turn based combat that gets extremely boring overtime. I've even managed to find the right builds to do clever combos, but it does not feel rewarding after clearing dungeons and beating bosses since all you get is just more of the same gameplay.

The only positives I'll give it, is it has a great artwork of a dark fantasy setting and has very good music, but that's about it.

A beautifully crafted and unforgiving dungeon crawler. The mechanic of your heroes having unique quirks and their mental/physical health having a significant effect on runs, the artstyle, and the [i] phenomenal [/i] performance of the narrator's VA make this a must play. I haven't played in a while but the time I had with this classic was unforgettable.

The game is way too reliant on chance and your heroes tweak out over a small paper cut. Would not recommend getting this game
